## Eduport

A single-user desktop app for tracking university applications, programs, people, labs, documents, and emails. Your data lives as plain Markdown files in a folder you choose — sync via Dropbox / iCloud / Syncthing, edit alongside Obsidian, never locked in.

[Project README](https://github.com/rusenbb/eduport/blob/main/README.md) · [Türkçe README](https://github.com/rusenbb/eduport/blob/main/README.tr.md)

## Download for your operating system

| Platform | File to grab | Quick install |
|---|---|---|
| **Windows 10 / 11** | `Eduport_*_x64-setup.exe` *(or the `.msi`)* | Double-click the file. If SmartScreen blocks it, click **More info → Run anyway**. |
| **macOS (Apple Silicon — M1, M2, M3, M4)** | `Eduport_*_aarch64.dmg` | Open the `.dmg`, drag **Eduport** into **Applications**. First launch: **right-click the app → Open** to dismiss the "unidentified developer" warning. |
| **Debian / Ubuntu / Mint** | `Eduport_*_amd64.deb` | In a terminal: `sudo apt install ./Eduport_*_amd64.deb` |
| **Fedora / RHEL / openSUSE** | `Eduport-*-x86_64.rpm` | In a terminal: `sudo dnf install ./Eduport-*-x86_64.rpm` *(or `zypper install` on openSUSE)* |

> **Heads up:** the installers aren't code-signed yet, so your OS will show a "from an unidentified developer" warning on first launch. The steps above explain how to dismiss it. Each asset's SHA-256 checksum is listed by GitHub below if you want to verify the download.

After install, the app walks you through a short onboarding: pick the folder where Eduport should keep your data, optionally seed it with a few sample entries, and you're in.
